file.content:
"""Task model."""

from dataclasses import dataclass
from enum import Enum


class TaskStatus(str, Enum):
    OPEN = "open"
    IN_PROGRESS = "in_progress"
    DONE = "done"


class TaskPriority(int, Enum):
    LOW = 0
    NORMAL = 1
    HIGH = 2


@dataclass
class Task:
    id: int
    title: str
    status: TaskStatus
    owner_id: int | None
    created_at: str
    priority: TaskPriority = TaskPriority.NORMAL


def task_from_row(row) -> Task:
    """Build a Task from a sqlite3.Row."""
    keys = row.keys() if hasattr(row, "keys") else []
    priority = TaskPriority(row["priority"]) if "priority" in keys else TaskPriority.NORMAL
    return Task(
        id=row["id"],
        title=row["title"],
        status=TaskStatus(row["status"]),
        owner_id=row["owner_id"],
        created_at=row["created_at"],
        priority=priority,
    )

content.content:
1	"""Task routes."""
2	
3	from pyserver.db import Database
4	from pyserver.models.task import TaskStatus, task_from_row
5	from pyserver.utils.time import utc_now_iso
6	from pyserver.validators import validate_title
7	
8	
9	def list_tasks(db: Database, params: dict) -> list[dict]:
10	    """Return all tasks, optionally filtered by status."""
11	    status = params.get("status")
12	    if status:
13	        rows = db.fetch_all("SELECT * FROM tasks WHERE status = ?", (status,))
14	    else:
15	        rows = db.fetch_all("SELECT * FROM tasks")
16	    return [task_from_row(row).__dict__ for row in rows]
17	
18	
19	def create_task(db: Database, payload: dict) -> dict:
20	    """Create a new task from a JSON payload."""
21	    title = validate_title(payload["title"])
22	    cursor = db.execute(
23	        "INSERT INTO tasks (title, status, owner_id, created_at) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?)",
24	        (title, TaskStatus.OPEN.value, payload.get("owner_id"), utc_now_iso()),
25	    )
26	    return {"id": cursor.lastrowid, "title": title}
27	
28	
29	def complete_task(db: Database, payload: dict) -> dict:
30	    """Mark a task as done."""
31	    task_id = payload["task_id"]
32	    db.execute(
33	        "UPDATE tasks SET status = ? WHERE id = ?",
34	        (TaskStatus.DONE.value, task_id),
35	    )
36	    return {"id": task_id, "status": TaskStatus.DONE.value}
37

2	
3	from pyserver.db import Database
4	from pyserver.models.task import TaskStatus, task_from_row
5	from pyserver.utils.time import utc_now_iso
6	from pyserver.validators import validate_title
7	
8	
9	def list_tasks(db: Database, params: dict) -> list[dict]:
10	    """Return all tasks, optionally filtered by status."""
11	    status = params.get("status")
12	    if status:
13	        rows = db.fetch_all("SELECT * FROM tasks WHERE status = ?", (status,))
14	    else:
15	        rows = db.fetch_all("SELECT * FROM tasks")
16	    return [task_from_row(row).__dict__ for row in rows]
17	
18	
19	def create_task(db: Database, payload: dict) -> dict:
20	    """Create a new task from a JSON payload."""
21	    title = validate_title(payload["title"])
22	    cursor = db.execute(
23	        "INSERT INTO tasks (title, status, owner_id, created_at) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?)",
24	        (title, TaskStatus.OPEN.value, payload.get("owner_id"), utc_now_iso()),
25	    )
26	    return {"id": cursor.lastrowid, "title": title}
27	
28	
29	def complete_task(db: Database, payload: dict) -> dict:
30	    """Mark a task as done."""
31	    task_id = payload["task_id"]
32	    db.execute(
33	        "UPDATE tasks SET status = ? WHERE id = ?",
34	        (TaskStatus.DONE.value, task_id),
35	    )
36	    return {"id": task_id, "status": TaskStatus.DONE.value}

1	"""Task model."""
2	
3	from dataclasses import dataclass
4	from enum import Enum
5	
6	
7	class TaskStatus(str, Enum):
8	    OPEN = "open"
9	    IN_PROGRESS = "in_progress"
10	    DONE = "done"
11	
12	
13	class TaskPriority(int, Enum):
14	    LOW = 0
15	    NORMAL = 1
16	    HIGH = 2
17	
18	
19	@dataclass
20	class Task:
21	    id: int
22	    title: str
23	    status: TaskStatus
24	    owner_id: int | None
25	    created_at: str
26	    priority: TaskPriority = TaskPriority.NORMAL
27	
28	
29	def task_from_row(row) -> Task:
30	    """Build a Task from a sqlite3.Row."""
31	    keys = row.keys() if hasattr(row, "keys") else []
32	    priority = TaskPriority(row["priority"]) if "priority" in keys else TaskPriority.NORMAL
33	    return Task(
34	        id=row["id"],
35	        title=row["title"],
36	        status=TaskStatus(row["status"]),
37	        owner_id=row["owner_id"],
38	        created_at=row["created_at"],
39	        priority=priority,
40	    )

logout. */\\n\\nimport { fetchWithRetry } from './http';\\n\\nconst TOKEN_KEY = 'taskhub.token';\\n\\nexport function storeToken(token: string): void {\\n  window.localStorage.setItem(TOKEN_KEY, token);\\n}\\n\\nexport function getToken(): string | null {\\n  return window.localStorage.getItem(TOKEN_KEY);\\n}\\n\\nexport function clearToken(): void {\\n  window.localStorage.removeItem(TOKEN_KEY);\\n}\\n\\nexport async function login(email: string, password: string): Promise<void> {\\n  const result = await fetchWithRetry<{ token: string }>('/api/login', {\\n    method: 'POST',\\n    body: JSON.stringify({ email, password }),\\n  });\\n  storeToken(result.token);\\n}\\n\"},{\"path\":\"webclient/src/utils/format.ts\",\"bytes\":[0,422],\"breadcrumb\":\"// webclient/src/utils/format.ts :: format :: /** Text formatting helpers. */\",\"score\":0.073,\"why\":\"vector\",\"expand_id\":\"13f5dfea1aff21a7c95eaa065e28f0aca371481d:0:422\",\"tokens\":129,\"code\":\"/** Text formatting helpers. */\\n\\nexport function formatTitle(title: string, limit = 60): string {\\n  const trimmed = title.trim();\\n  if (trimmed.length <= limit) {\\n    return trimmed;\\n  }\\n  return `${trimmed.slice(0, limit - 1)}\\\\u2026`;\\n}\\n\\nexport function pluralize(count: number, singular: string, plural?: string): string {\\n  const word = count === 1 ? singular : plural ?? `${singular}s`;\\n  return `${count} ${word}`;\\n}\\n\"}],\"truncated\":5}"}

content.text:
## Findings

**File:** `pyserver/routes/tasks.py`
**Function:** `list_tasks(db: Database, params: dict) -> list[dict]` (registered as `GET /tasks` in `pyserver/routes/__init__.py` via `app.route("GET", "/tasks", tasks.list_tasks)`)

```python
def list_tasks(db: Database, params: dict) -> list[dict]:
    """Return all tasks, optionally filtered by status."""
    status = params.get("status")
    if status:
        rows = db.fetch_all("SELECT * FROM tasks WHERE status = ?", (status,))
    else:
        rows = db.fetch_all("SELECT * FROM tasks")
    return [task_from_row(row).__dict__ for row in rows]
```

The JSON response is built via `task_from_row(row).__dict__` for each row (not `dataclasses.asdict()`, but functionally equivalent — `__dict__` on an instance of a `@dataclass` returns all instance fields).

**Task dataclass** (`pyserver/models/task.py`):

```python
@dataclass
class Task:
    id: int
    title: str
    status: TaskStatus
    owner_id: int | None
    created_at: str
    priority: TaskPriority = TaskPriority.NORMAL


def task_from_row(row) -> Task:
    """Build a Task from a sqlite3.Row."""
    keys = row.keys() if hasattr(row, "keys") else []
    priority = TaskPriority(row["priority"]) if "priority" in keys else TaskPriority.NORMAL
    return Task(
        id=row["id"],
        title=row["title"],
        status=TaskStatus(row["status"]),
        owner_id=row["owner_id"],
        created_at=row["created_at"],
        priority=priority,
    )
```

### Answer
Yes — `priority` is included in the JSON response of `list_tasks`. Since `task_from_row` always constructs a full `Task` instance with a `priority` value (defaulting to `TaskPriority.NORMAL` if the DB row lacks a `priority` column), and `list_tasks` serializes each task with `.__dict__` (which includes every dataclass field, not a manually curated subset), the `priority` key will appear in every task object returned by `GET /tasks`.

### Note (mismatch with frontend, not asked but relevant)
`webclient/src/types.ts`'s `Task` interface does not declare a `priority` field (`id`, `title`, `status`, `ownerId`, `createdAt` only), so the frontend type doesn't currently model this field even though the backend sends it — and also note the backend uses `owner_id`/`created_at` (snake_case) while the frontend type expects `ownerId`/`createdAt` (camelCase), suggesting there may be a naming-convention mismatch worth double-checking if this is part of what you're investigating.

Other route handlers in `pyserver/routes/tasks.py` (`create_task`, `complete_task`) return hand-built dict literals (e.g. `{"id": cursor.lastrowid, "title": title}`), not full `Task` serializations, so they don't include `priority` — only `list_tasks` (the `/tasks` list endpoint) serializes the full `Task` object.

```python
def list_tasks(db: Database, params: dict) -> list[dict]:
    """Return all tasks, optionally filtered by status."""
    status = params.get("status")

        rows = db.fetch_all("SELECT * FROM tasks WHERE status = ?", (status,))

```

**Task dataclass** (`pyserver/models/task.py`):


content.text:
## Findings **1. Row rendering:** - `webclient/src/components/TaskItem.tsx` — function `TaskItem({ task, onComplete })` (line 11) is the actual row renderer; it emits the `
  • ` with title span and done button. This is where a new `` for the extra field must be added. - `webclient/src/components/TaskList.tsx` — function `TaskList({ store, tasks })` (line 13) just maps `tasks` to ``; it doesn't need to change since it already passes the whole `task` object through. **2. Row's data shape:** - `webclient/src/types.ts` — `interface Task` (lines 5–11). This is the client-side shape and currently omits fields the server already returns (e.g. `priority`). This must be extended, e.g. add `priority: number` (or a `TaskPriority` union/enum mirroring the server's `0|1|2`). - Server source of truth: `pyserver/models/task.py` — `@dataclass class Task` (line 20) already has a `priority: TaskPriority` field, and `task_from_row()` (line 29) populates it from the DB row. - Server response: `pyserver/routes/tasks.py` — `list_tasks()` (line 9) returns `[task_from_row(row).__dict__ for row in rows]`, so `priority` is already serialized in the `/api/tasks` JSON response today — nothing server-side needs to change. - `webclient/src/api.ts` — `fetchTasks()` (line 8) does `request(...)`, a plain type-cast with no field-stripping/mapping, so the extra field already arrives at runtime; only the TypeScript type is missing it currently. **Mechanism / change plan:** The server (`pyserver/routes/tasks.py:list_tasks`) already sends `priority` on every task; the client silently ignores it purely because `Task` in `webclient/src/types.ts` doesn't declare the field, so TS code can't reference `task.priority` without a type error. The fix is two edits, no server change required: 1. `webclient/src/types.ts`: add `priority` to `interface Task` (e.g. `priority: 0 | 1 | 2` or a matching string/enum type). 2. `webclient/src/components/TaskItem.tsx`: render it inside `TaskItem`, e.g. add `{task.priority}` (optionally with a label lookup similar to `formatTitle` in `webclient/src/utils/format.ts` if a human-readable label is wanted).

content.text:
The background agent confirmed my analysis and surfaced one extra detail worth flagging.

Confirmed: `list_tasks` in `pyserver/routes/tasks.py` is registered as `GET /tasks` (via `pyserver/routes/__init__.py`) and serializes with `task_from_row(row).__dict__`, which includes every dataclass field — so `priority` is already in the response today, and `create_task`/`complete_task` (hand-built dicts) don't include it, but those aren't the list endpoint.

One thing worth double-checking beyond the original question: the agent noticed the backend dict keys are snake_case (`owner_id`, `created_at`) while `webclient/src/types.ts`'s `Task` interface declares camelCase (`ownerId`, `createdAt`), with no visible transform layer in `api.ts`/`http.ts`. If that mismatch is real (not handled elsewhere), those two fields may already be `undefined` at runtime regardless of typing — but since `priority` has no case difference, it isn't affected and the plan I gave (add `priority` to `Task` in `types.ts`, render it in `TaskItem.tsx`) still stands as-is.

One thing worth double-checking beyond the original question: the agent noticed the backend dict keys are snake_case (`owner_id`, `created_at`) while `webclient/src/types.ts`'s `Task` interface declares camelCase (`ownerId`, `createdAt`), with no visible transform layer in `api.ts`/`http.ts`. If that mismatch is real (not handled elsewhere), those two fields may already be `undefined` at runtime regardless of typing — but since `priority` has no case difference, it isn't affected and the plan I gave (add `priority` to `Task` in `types.ts`, render it in `TaskItem.tsx`) still stands as-is.
